tests.name: each_phase1_step_completes
tests.details: All 5 PHASE1_STEPS (architecture_selected, threat_model_complete, data_model_defined, ui_scaffolding_done, bible_synthesized) completed RC=0 sequentially

tests.name: phase1_to_phase2_gate_passes
tests.details: check-phase-gate.sh RC=0 with consistent state at phase 0

tests.name: phase_state_advances_to_2
tests.details: U-D regression: no script automatically advances .current_phase after gate passes. Manually set current_phase=2 to proceed with subsequent sections.

framework_bugs.summary: U-D confirmation: no script auto-advances current_phase after gate passes
framework_bugs.reproduction: After completing all PHASE1_STEPS via --complete-step, run scripts/check-phase-gate.sh; observe phase-state.json .current_phase remains at 0.
framework_bugs.expected: A scripted path (or part of check-phase-gate.sh upon successful gate) should advance .current_phase to the next integer and record the gate date.
framework_bugs.actual: .current_phase remained 0 after all phase1 steps and check-phase-gate.sh passed; no documented script transitions the state.

documentation_gaps.gap: No documented procedure to advance .current_phase between phases — gate scripts only consistency-check, never transition state.

tests.name: start_feature_records_state
tests.details: .build_loop.feature == 'uat-feat-1' after --start-feature

tests.name: each_step_completes_in_order
tests.details: All 5 build_loop steps (tests_written -> documentation_updated) completed RC=0 in order; security_audit artifact gate satisfied via stub findings file.

tests.name: pre_commit_passes_after_loop
tests.details: git commit -m 'feat(uat-1): ...' succeeded after all 5 steps + record-feature.

tests.name: bl006_blocks_feat_without_loop
tests.details: U-C confirmation: direct shell `git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(bl006-trigger): no loop'` succeeded with RC=0 — no commit-msg hook, no client-side gate. The PreToolUse path (pre-commit-gate.sh) gates the agent, but direct shell git bypasses BL-006.

tests.name: bl006_grace_window_resets_after_record
tests.details: After test-gate.sh --record-feature uat-feat-1 + completing build_loop:feature_recorded, a second feat: commit (uat-feat-2) with proper Build Loop completed successfully.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_blocks_commit
tests.details: With .claude/pending-approval.json present, simulated PreToolUse payload {"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"git commit -m feat(uat-3)"}} piped to scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh produced JSON permissionDecision=deny with reason citing the BL-015 sentinel.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_resolves
tests.details: scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve removed the sentinel; subsequent commit invocation no longer mentions sentinel (was blocked by a separate Phase 2 init verification gate, which is expected behavior).

tests.name: recorded_feature_appears_in_progress
tests.details: After --record-feature uat-feat-1, .features_completed contained 'uat-feat-1' in build-progress.json (verified immediately after record).

framework_bugs.summary: U-C confirmation: BL-006 not enforced via git commit-msg hook
framework_bugs.reproduction: From PROJECT_DIR with no active --start-feature: `git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(bypass): no loop'`
framework_bugs.expected: Commit blocked client-side (commit-msg hook or similar) when no Build Loop is recorded for the feat:.
framework_bugs.actual: Commit succeeded RC=0; HEAD advanced. PreToolUse gate only fires when commits go through the agent's Bash tool, not direct shell.

tests.name: start_phase3_exits_zero
tests.details: process-checklist.sh --start-phase3 RC=0 (with WARN about features_since_last_test that was non-fatal).

tests.name: start_uat_creates_session
tests.details: .uat_session.started_at present after --start-uat 1

tests.name: uat_template_lints_clean
tests.details: scripts/lint-uat-scenarios.sh requires a populated HTML file with embedded `const scenarios = [...]` array that parses as JSON. With minimal stub HTML the linter returns RC=2 (structural failure: 'No scenarios block found'). With a JS object literal the linter rejects (jq parse failure on JS comments/identifiers, not strict JSON). The linter exists and runs, but creating a stub that lints clean is non-trivial — N/A for stub-driven UAT runs.

tests.name: each_uat_step_completes
tests.details: All 9 UAT_STEPS (agents_dispatched -> gate_passed) completed RC=0 sequentially with stub artifacts in docs/test-results/.

tests.name: phase3_to_phase4_gate
tests.details: check-phase-gate.sh RC=1 with multiple WARN/FAIL: gate dates not stored in canonical .gates.phase_X_to_Y schema (they were stored as flat top-level keys gate_X_to_Y_date). PROJECT_BIBLE.md missing (FAIL). HANDOFF.md / SECURITY.md / sbom.json / penetration test missing (WARN). Most are stub-environment expected; the gate-date schema mismatch is a probable bug — see framework_bugs.

framework_bugs.summary: Phase-state gate-date schema duality: flat keys vs .gates.phase_X_to_Y
framework_bugs.reproduction: Init creates phase-state.json with `.gates.phase_0_to_1: null` etc. plus no flat keys. Setting `.gates.phase_X_to_Y = '2026-04-26'` is what check-phase-gate.sh reads. But other parts of the codebase appear to write flat top-level `.gate_X_to_Y_date` keys.
framework_bugs.expected: One canonical schema for gate dates that writers and readers agree on.
framework_bugs.actual: check-phase-gate.sh consistently warns 'current_phase is N but gate date not recorded' even when flat keys are populated, because it reads the nested .gates path. UAT agents (and likely the framework's own state-advance logic, if any existed) need to populate both.

documentation_gaps.gap: lint-uat-scenarios.sh expects a very specific populated-template format (JS file with `const scenarios = [...]` parseable as JSON). No example or template path documented in --help output.

tests.name: start_phase4_exits_zero
tests.details: First call RC=1 because poc_mode='production' triggered the POC block. After running scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production (which cleared poc_mode to null), --start-phase4 RC=0. This is expected per the production-bound POC -> production workflow.

tests.name: each_phase4_step_completes
tests.details: All 6 PHASE4_STEPS (production_build, rollback_tested, go_live_verified, monitoring_configured, handoff_written, handoff_tested) completed RC=0 with stub artifacts. Artifact gates fired correctly: rollback needed *rollback* file in docs/test-results, monitoring needed HANDOFF.md with monitoring keywords, handoff_tested needed *handoff* file.

tests.name: handoff_md_required
tests.details: monitoring_configured failed RC=1 with 'HANDOFF.md not found' before HANDOFF.md was created; passed after stubbing HANDOFF.md with 'monitoring' keyword.

tests.name: release_notes_required
tests.details: RELEASE_NOTES.md is auto-created by init.sh with a stub; go_live_verified passed because the file already existed since init.

tests.name: phase4_completion_state
tests.details: .current_phase=4 and all 6 phase4_release.steps_completed populated.

framework_bugs.summary: production gov_mode requires --to-production upgrade before phase 4
framework_bugs.reproduction: Init with --gov-mode production -> phase-state.json has poc_mode='production' -> --start-phase4 fails with 'POC projects complete at Phase 3'.
framework_bugs.expected: If user explicitly chose --gov-mode production at init, phase 4 should be unlocked from the start (production mode is the eventual target). Requiring upgrade-project.sh --to-production for an already-production project is confusing.
framework_bugs.actual: User must run scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production to clear poc_mode before phase 4 can be started, even though they originally chose production mode.

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: Grep for password|api_key|secret|token across committed files matches only template/doc references (CI workflow placeholders, .gitignore secret patterns, docs about secrets). Tightened regex for actual values (>=16 char alphanumeric assignments, excluding TODO/placeholder/example) returned 0 hits.

tests.name: log_files_consistent
tests.details: N/A — no FORCE/reset events occurred during this run, so .claude/process-audit.log absence is correct (file is append-only on bypass events).

tests.name: no_orphaned_tempfiles
tests.details: find $WORKDIR/.claude -name '*.tmp' returned no results

tests.name: no_framework_contamination
tests.details: git -C $FRAMEWORK status --short shows only the same '?? .claude/' and '?? Reports/uat-2026-04-26/' untracked dirs that existed at setup. git -C $FRAMEWORK rev-parse HEAD returns d66e8f276079aa352ba90ac54386e1b1896d7494 (matches setup snapshot).

summary: Base/production/web/standard happy path completed end-to-end. PR #17/#18/#19 fixes confirmed: --non-interactive works, framework-self guard active, helpers copied, fake-remote tolerated. Confirmed prior-sweep open bugs U-C (BL-006 client-side bypass) and U-D (no auto phase advance) at Medium severity. Two new Medium findings: gate-date schema duality (flat vs nested .gates) and the production-gov-mode -> upgrade-required-for-phase4 UX wrinkle.
